questions.question: What happens if you delete a file from a USB flash drive in Windows OS?
questions.option_1: It is compressed to save space.
questions.option_3: It goes to the Recycle Bin.

questions.question: What is the default name given to a folder on Windows systems?
Which of the following is an example of an open-source desktop Operating System?
Which of the following scenarios would most likely result in a 'page fault' error during typical computer operation?
questions.option_1: Accessing data stored in the CPU cache
questions.option_2: Writing data to a read-only memory location
questions.option_3: Attempting to access a memory address that has been swapped out to the hard drive
questions.option_4: Executing an instruction directly from the BIOS

questions.question: Which of the following scheduling algorithms is most prone to convoy effect, where a long-running process blocks shorter processes behind it?
Which of the following Operating System concepts is most directly involved in ensuring that multiple processes can access and modify files concurrently without causing data corruption?
Which of the following operating systems provides the highest level of user control over system resources and is most commonly used for professional software development on desktops and laptops?
Which of the following operating systems is most suitable for ethical hacking and cybersecurity tasks on laptops and desktops due to its pre-installed penetration testing tools?
Which output device is specifically designed to produce high-quality printed graphics and text?
Which of the following devices processes data in the computer system?
Which of the following options in PowerPoint allows you to see all the slides at once as thumbnails?
Which of the following is the correct way to start a new blank presentation in MS PowerPoint?
Which of the following is the correct way to add a new slide in an MS-PowerPoint 2019 presentation?
questions.option_3: Go to the Home tab → Click New Slide

questions.question: Which of the following MS PowerPoint features allows you to record the timing for each slide and any narration or laser pointer movements to be replayed automatically during a slideshow?
In a multitasking operating system, what role does RAM primarily play when multiple applications are open simultaneously?
questions.option_1: It queues printing tasks before they reach the printer.
questions.option_2: It temporarily holds active program data and instructions to enable fast switching between tasks.
questions.option_3: It acts as permanent storage for installed software.
questions.option_4: It processes user input commands from external devices like keyboard or mouse.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about RAM and ROM with reference to computer memory is correct?
questions.option_1: RAM is non-volatile, while ROM is volatile.
questions.option_2: RAM cannot be written to, only read from.
questions.option_4: RAM temporarily stores data for active programs, while ROM permanently stores firmware.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the role of RAM and ROM in a computer system?
questions.option_1: ROM is used for storing data that can be modified and written to, while RAM stores fixed data.
questions.option_2: RAM is used to store the operating system and permanent files, while ROM is used to store temporary data.
questions.option_3: ROM is used to store system firmware, while RAM stores temporary data for active processes.
questions.option_4: RAM is non-volatile, while ROM is volatile.

questions.question: In the context of computer hardware components, what is the primary function of ROM?
questions.option_1: It physically supports all internal hardware modules.
questions.option_2: It executes programs and handles arithmetic operations.
questions.option_3: It stores temporary data used during system processing.
questions.option_4: It holds essential firmware required for booting the system.

questions.question: Which of the following shortcut keys can be used to quickly open a presentation in PowerPoint?
What is the purpose of the Slide Master in MS PowerPoint?
questions.option_2: To apply consistent formatting, fonts, and layouts to all slides
questions.option_3: To delete multiple slides at once

questions.question: What happens if you try to open a MS PowerPoint presentation created in a newer version (e.g., MS PowerPoint 2021) in an older version (e.g., MS PowerPoint 2010)?
questions.option_1: The file will be automatically converted to a PDF.
questions.option_2: The file will open normally with all features intact.
questions.option_3: The file will open in Compatibility Mode with limited features.
questions.option_4: MS PowerPoint will display an error and refuse to open the file.

questions.question: What is the difference between dragging text with the mouse (left-click) and Ctrl + dragging text in MS Word 365?
questions.option_1: Dragging deletes the text; Ctrl + dragging pastes it.
questions.option_2: Ctrl + dragging changes the font style.
questions.option_3: Dragging moves the text; Ctrl + dragging copies it.

questions.question: What happens if you paste text into MS-PowerPoint 2019 with the Keep Source Formatting option?
questions.option_1: The text adopts the destination slide’s theme fonts/colors.
questions.option_2: The text retains its original formatting from the source.
questions.option_3: The text is converted into an image.
questions.option_4: The text size automatically adjusts to fit the slide.

questions.question: What is the shortcut to open an MS PowerPoint file directly from the application?
What does setting a Page Break in MS Excel 365 allow you to control?
questions.option_1: Whether formulas are recalculated before printing
questions.option_2: The maximum size of printable margins
questions.option_3: Where headers appear on each page
questions.option_4: Where the worksheet splits across printed pages
